content.fundsUsageStatement: Acquisition of 3 new electric delivery vans to service a recently awarded municipal contract.

guarantor.title: Mr
guarantor.totalPersonalDebtNote: Credit cards paid in full monthly. No other personal debt.

directorships.companyName: Miller Holdings Who Love to Hold Things Forever Ltd

flags.title: Implausible Monthly Housing Costs
flags.expectedValue: A value consistent with UK housing costs and less than annual income
flags.description: The stated monthly housing cost (£66,350) is biologically and financially impossible given the guarantor's income and UK norms.
flags.recommendation: Request immediate clarification and correction of the monthly housing cost figure.
flags.reasoning: A monthly housing cost of £66,350 would total nearly £800,000 per year, which is not plausible for a personal residence in the UK and far exceeds the guarantor's annual income. This violates hard sanity invariants and undermines the credibility of the affordability assessment.

flags.title: Operating Expenses Exceed Turnover
flags.expectedValue: Less than or equal to average monthly turnover
flags.description: Operating expenses (£35,000/month) are significantly higher than the reported average monthly turnover (£6,000/month).
flags.recommendation: Request clarification and supporting documentation for both turnover and operating expenses.
flags.reasoning: A business cannot sustainably operate with expenses far exceeding turnover. This discrepancy suggests either a data entry error or a fundamental misunderstanding of the business's financial position, violating basic financial logic and likely policy on affordability.

flags.title: Low Average Monthly Turnover Relative to Requested Loan
flags.expectedValue: A value proportionate to a £120,000 loan request
flags.description: The average monthly turnover (£6,000) is low compared to the requested loan amount (£120,000).
flags.recommendation: Request recent management accounts or bank statements to verify turnover.
flags.reasoning: Policy typically requires that loan amounts are proportionate to business turnover. A £120,000 loan against £72,000 annual turnover (if figures are correct) is likely outside policy limits for loan-to-turnover ratios.

flags.title: Guarantor FICO Score at Policy Minimum
flags.description: Guarantor's FICO score is at the minimum acceptable threshold.
flags.recommendation: Proceed with caution and consider additional risk mitigants.
flags.reasoning: A FICO score of 620 is typically the minimum for most business loan policies. While not a violation, it indicates higher risk and may require compensating factors.

triage.reasoning: While the business and guarantor appear stable and creditworthy, the affordability section contains biologically and logically impossible values (e.g., monthly housing costs exceeding annual income and turnover far below operating expenses), which violate hard sanity invariants and undermine the reliability of the proposal. These issues must be resolved before any further consideration.
triage.summary: The proposal is generally strong, with a long-established business, fair credit score, and no derogatory credit marks. However, there are critical inconsistencies in the affordability section, specifically the implausibly high monthly housing costs and low average monthly turnover relative to operating expenses, which raise significant concerns about data accuracy and affordability.
triage.recommendation: Decline pending clarification of affordability figures and correction of data inconsistencies.
